name: The Bridges of Vinalopo (PR-CV 169) — Circular from Aspe
name_original: Los Puentes del Vinalopó (PR-CV 169) — Circular desde Aspe
region_intro: Hiking around Aspe offers diverse experiences through its natural landscapes and well-maintained routes. The region is situated in the Vinalopó River valley, featuring riverine environments and mountainous terrain. Notable areas include Los Algezares Municipal Natural Landscape with its canyon-like formations and historic sites, and the Sierra de la Ofra, known for its preserved wooded areas. These varied features provide a range of hiking opportunities for different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Aspe The most popular hiking route is The Bridges of Vinalopo (PR-CV 169) — Circular from Aspe, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 30 minutes to complete. This route allows hikers to experience the landscape around the Vinalopó River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Elche Reservoir Viewpoint – Five-Arch Bridge loop from plaza de Las Palmeras, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path. This trail features views of the Elche Reservoir and the historic Five-Arch Bridge. Local hikers also love the Five-Arch Bridge – Bridge of the 4 Eyes loop from Embalse d’Elx, a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through historic bridges and reservoir surroundings, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Aspe is defined by river valleys, mountainous terrain, and canyon-like landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Aspe are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 180 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Aspe's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Aspe?
A: Aspe offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 25 routes available. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, ensuring options for all skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Aspe?
A: The terrain around Aspe is quite diverse, featuring river valleys, mountainous areas, and unique canyon-like landscapes. You'll encounter the lush scenery of the Vinalopó River valley, the striking rock formations of Los Algezares Municipal Natural Landscape, and the wooded areas of Sierra de la Ofra.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Aspe?
A: Yes, Aspe is home to several circular routes. A notable one is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e863340859/the-bridges-of-vinalopo-pr-cv-169-circular-from-aspe" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Bridges of Vinalopo (PR-CV 169) — Circular from Aspe&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to experience the landscape around the Vinalopó River.

Q: Are there any hikes with waterfalls or natural pools in the area?
A: Yes, within the Los Algezares Municipal Natural Landscape, you can discover a natural pool. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10326291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Five-Arch Bridge – Elche Reservoir and Waterfall loop from Embalse de Elche&lt;/a&gt; specifically features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2440117" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elche Reservoir and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque views.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Aspe?
A: Absolutely. Aspe has 5 easy routes suitable for families. The area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7097250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elche Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is particularly noted as a picturesque and family-friendly spot for walks and picnics.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see on hikes around Aspe?
A: Hikers can explore a rich array of natural features and historical landmarks. Highlights include the canyon-like formations and old tunnels in Los Algezares, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6891285" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Five-Arch Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and Bridge of the 4 Eyes, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7097250" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elche Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts the Vinalopó River valley and the preserved woodlands of Sierra de la Ofra.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Aspe?
A: The hiking routes in Aspe are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 180 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountainous terrain, and the well-maintained paths that offer varied experiences.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Aspe?
A: While specific regulations for dogs on all trails are not detailed, many natural areas in Spain are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. It's always recommended to ensure your dog is well-behaved and to clean up after them to maintain the natural environment for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Aspe?
A: The best time to hike in Aspe is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the weather is pleasant. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons more suitable for hiking, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there challenging hikes available in Aspe?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, Aspe offers 2 difficult routes. One example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10326291" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Five-Arch Bridge – Elche Reservoir and Waterfall loop from Embalse de Elche&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a more strenuous experience with rewarding views.

Q: Where can I park for hiking trails in Aspe?
A: While specific parking areas for every trailhead are not listed, Aspe is a well-established town. You can typically find parking within the town itself or at designated spots near popular natural attractions like the Elche Reservoir or the Los Algezares area. Always check local signage for parking restrictions.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the hiking trails in Aspe?
A: Aspe is a town with various amenities, so you'll find restaurants, cafes, and accommodation options within the town center. Many trailheads are accessible from or near the town, making it convenient to find refreshments and lodging before or after your hike.
tips: Known as "4 Eyes" for its 4 arches that make up its structure, it has great historical and architectural value for what it represents and stands out in the Elche area.
